blockingIssues.description: Canonical rename was left incomplete: active Solidity still imports old interfaces/names and the old files `LendingCoreV2.sol`, `RiskAdapter.sol`, and `LendingRouterV2.sol` remain in `dualvm/contracts/`.

The Foundry governance migration dropped execute/version-activation coverage: `GovernorLifecycle.t.sol` stops at `queue()` and no longer proves governance `execute()` plus `MarketVersionRegistry.activateVersion()` behavior required by the validation contract.
`dualvm/package-lock.json` still keeps root Hardhat dependencies, so clean installs via `npm ci` are not actually Foundry-only.
The cleanup deleted `dualvm/scripts/gasBenchmark.ts` without a replacement, removing requested live-ops benchmarking capability.
`Deploy.s.sol` never implemented the required WPAS/USDC asset reuse or override path; it always deploys fresh assets, so the script misses the requested idempotent asset behavior.
The reviewed PVM deployment wrapper was not end-to-end usable because `DeployPVM.sh` instructed operators to pass a quote-engine env var that the reviewed `Deploy.s.sol` did not actually consume.
The canonical deployment reused an M9 PVM `DeterministicRiskModel` instead of freshly deploying it via `resolc` in this feature run, so the deployment evidence does not match the feature scope.
The canonical deployment script hardcodes zero execution delays for sensitive AccessManager roles, violating the mission's non-zero delay posture for risk, treasury, minter, and related operations.
The live proof never required `QuoteVerified`/successful PVM verification during borrow; it could succeed while only observing `CrossVMDivergence`.
`XcmInbox.receiveReceipt()` was exercised with a simulated liquidation correlation ID instead of the real on-chain liquidation ID, so the proof did not close the actual bilateral async trace.
The proof replaced the required duplicate-receipt live revert with a read-only `hasProcessed()` check, so `DuplicateCorrelationId` was not actually proven on testnet.
The live liquidation/XCM proof only logged whether `LiquidationNotified` happened and allowed the proof to pass even when the notifier path failed.
The wrapper makes the event-correlator step non-fatal (`|| true`), so the feature can report success without the required correlator output artifact.

`SYSTEM_ARCHITECTURE_AUDIT.md` still contains multiple contradictory pre-M11 sections (`LendingCore`, `RiskAdapter`, stale issue table/ASCII board), so the architecture audit is not consistently updated to the canonical bilateral async system.
`README.md` still contains multiple legacy `LendingCore` / `RiskAdapter` references outside the refreshed architecture section, so it is not consistently M11-accurate.
`BILATERAL_ASYNC_ARCHITECTURE.md` remained pre-M11/pre-Foundry-only even though the feature explicitly required it to be verified as current.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Replace Hardhat-era deployment instructions with the repo's Foundry-first workflow (`forge build`, `forge test`, `forge script`, `cast` verification, deploy profile/flags), and add a required post-broadcast receipt re-fetch step for any proof that depends on block-derived IDs such as `correlationId`.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both `canonical-testnet-deployment` and `bilateral-async-proof` reviews found the active deployment procedure already relies on Foundry/cast rather than Hardhat, and the bilateral proof review showed simulation-derived correlation IDs diverging from final on-chain values.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the docs-worker validation procedure to use the repo's canonical validator commands from `.factory/services.yaml` (Foundry-first) instead of legacy `npm test` / `npm run build` defaults.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `architecture-docs-update` review found the docs-worker skill still prescribes npm-centric validation even though M11 repo guidance and services are now `forge test`, `forge build`, and `forge fmt --check`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add an M11 Foundry migration gotchas section covering: updating all `vm.expectEmit` sites when event signatures change, handling Foundry manifest/log censoring for deployment outputs, and allowing a lighter validation path for formatting-only fixes.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `foundry-migrate-peripheral-tests`, `forge-deploy-script`, and `forge-fmt-fix` reviews each found worker-skill gaps around event-signature migrations, manifest-writing/censoring behavior, and style-only validation scope.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Normalize mission guidance to the canonical M11 names and toolchain only: remove contradictory `LendingCore`/`RiskAdapter`/Hardhat-era references and align all guidance with `LendingEngine`, `RiskGateway`, `LendingRouter`, and Foundry-only operations.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `foundry-migrate-core-tests` and `architecture-docs-update` reviews both found mission guidance and shared references mixing pre-M11 and post-M11 terminology, which likely contributed to incomplete renames and stale docs.

rejectedObservations.observation: Update `.factory/library/architecture.md`, `.factory/library/environment.md`, and `.factory/library/user-testing.md` immediately for every stale M11 naming/toolchain reference.
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate: multiple reviews reported the same stale-library drift, and the core guidance problem is already captured in the AGENTS/skill recommendations above rather than mutating several shared knowledge files during a failing scrutiny round.

rejectedObservations.observation: Remove the legacy Hardhat commands from `.factory/services.yaml` right now.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous: the same scrutiny round found the cleanup feature itself incomplete (`package-lock.json` still retains Hardhat dependencies), so changing the service catalog now would mask rather than document the mismatch until the underlying cleanup fix lands.

rejectedObservations.observation: Patch mission bookkeeping to backfill `completedWorkerSessionId` for `canonical-testnet-deployment`.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous: this is mission-state bookkeeping rather than a low-risk services/library fact update, and it should be handled by the orchestrator workflow that owns feature state transitions.
